name: modal
title: Modal
description: A focus-trapped overlay panel for tasks that require the user's full attention. Use for forms, multi-step flows, or detail views that interrupt the page until explicitly dismissed.

meta.import: import {ComposedModal, Modal, ModalBody, ModalFooter, ModalHeader} from '@camunda/design-system';
meta.docs:
# Modal

A focus-trapped overlay panel for tasks that require the user's full attention. Use for forms, multi-step flows, or detail views that interrupt the page until explicitly dismissed.

## When to use

- **Forms or multi-step flows** — compose `Dialog` + `DialogTrigger` + `DialogContent` with `DialogHeader` + `DialogFooter`. Use `<DialogClose asChild>` for the cancel button.
- **Title and description** — use `<DialogTitle>` and `<DialogDescription>` inside `<DialogHeader>` for a11y-correct labelling.
- **Trigger from any element** — wrap any button, link, or custom element in `<DialogTrigger asChild>`.
- **Custom width** — restyle `<DialogContent className="sm:max-w-md / 2xl / 4xl">`. Default is `sm:max-w-lg`.
- **Prevent outside-click dismissal** — `<DialogContent onPointerDownOutside={(e) => e.preventDefault()}>`.
- **Custom focus on open** — `<DialogContent onOpenAutoFocus={(e) => { e.preventDefault(); ref.current?.focus() }}>`.

## When not to use

- **Destructive confirmation** ("Delete?", "Discard changes?") — use `AlertDialog`. It defaults focus to the cancel button, blocks outside-click, and uses `role="alertdialog"`.
- **Transient feedback** ("Saved", "Copied") — use `Sonner` toasts.
- **Inline content that doesn't need to interrupt** — render in place.
- **Picking from a list of options** — use `Popover` or `DropdownMenu`.

## Composition

- shadcn ships **one** API (`Dialog` family) — no separate `Modal` vs. `ComposedModal`. Always compose explicitly.
- The close (X) button is built into `<DialogContent>`. There is no `passiveModal` prop — to omit footer buttons, just don't render `<DialogFooter>`.
- For `loadingStatus`-style pending state, render a `<Spinner />` next to the action button and disable it while pending.
meta.migrationGuide: # Modal — Carbon → shadcn migration guide ## Two Carbon APIs, one shadcn API Carbon ships **two** parallel modal APIs: - **`Modal`** — monolithic, prop-driven (`modalHeading`, `modalLabel`, `primaryButtonText`, `secondaryButtonText`, `onRequestSubmit`, `onRequestClose`). - **`ComposedModal`** + `ModalHeader` + `ModalBody` + `ModalFooter` — explicit composition where each piece is a separate component. shadcn ships **one** API: `Dialog` family, always composed: ``` {trigger} … … {body} ``` Both Carbon `Modal` and `ComposedModal` migrate to the same shadcn `Dialog` shape. The `ComposedModal` migration is more direct (1:1 component mapping); the `Modal` migration replaces props with explicit children. ## Decision rule: Dialog vs. AlertDialog Carbon's `` (or destructive `ComposedModal`) is for "are you sure?" confirmations. In shadcn, **the right primitive for those is `AlertDialog`**, not `Dialog`. The differences: | | shadcn `Dialog` | shadcn `AlertDialog` | |---|---|---| | Use case | general modal (forms, info, multi-step) | destructive / blocking confirmation | | Built-in cancel button | no — render `` manually | yes — `` auto-closes | | Built-in action button | no — render any `` in `` | | `secondaryButtonText` | `` | | `danger` | switch to `AlertDialog` (or use ``. 7. Replace `secondaryButtonText` with `` — `DialogClose` auto-closes the dialog. 8. For `passiveModal`: omit ``. 9. For `size`: restyle via ``. 10. For `preventCloseOnClickOutside`: ` e.preventDefault()}>`. 11. For `selectorPrimaryFocus`: ` { e.preventDefault(); ref.current?.focus() }}>`. 12. For `ComposedModal` family: 1:1 mapping (`ModalHeader` → `DialogHeader`+`DialogTitle`, `ModalBody` → bare children, `ModalFooter` → `DialogFooter` with explicit buttons). 13. If you used `loadingStatus`: render a `` next to the action button and disable it while pending. 14. **For destructive (`danger`) modals: switch to `AlertDialog`** — it has the right semantics (`role="alertdialog"`, no outside-click dismiss, defaults focus to cancel).
